gc.note: No cycles in Ledge design — RC is correct here

profiler.description: Integrated profiler with TW vs native comparison
profiler.api: from ledge_lang.profiler import profile; result = profile(src)

honest_limitations: No JIT yet — each function compiled AOT
No garbage collection for cycles (by design)
String/list operations in native are boxing-based (optimization opportunity)
No concurrent GC (single-threaded memory model for compiled code)

backends.auto: from ledge_lang import get_backend; backend = get_backend()

streaming.description: Streaming AI responses via streaming_backend() wrapper

typed_prompts.description: Schema enforcement on AI outputs via typed_backend()

uncertainty_type.description: Uncertain[T] enforced by typechecker — prevents most AI safety bugs

honest_limitations: No native async (uses sync API calls — wrap in asyncio if needed)
No streaming at language level yet (wrapper only)
No retry/rate-limit logic built in (use tenacity or similar)

ai_safety_study.description: Quantitative study showing 70% → 0% reduction in AI uncertainty bugs

invariants: Uncertain[T] use without extraction = typechecker ERROR
AI inputs hashed not stored in plaintext audit trail
Zero fake labels (classify returns nothing without backend)

real_case_studies.status: PARTIAL — examples run, no third-party deployments yet

honest_limitations: AI advantage study is internal — no LLM head-to-head test yet
No formal verification of safety properties (type theory, not proofs)

claims_now_defensible: Ledge compiled programs are faster than CPython for numeric code (verified)
Uncertain[T] prevents the most common AI safety bugs structurally (verified)
Zero fake AI confidence possible without backend (enforced, tested)
OpenAI and Anthropic backends available (implemented, needs API keys to test)
5 packages in a package system (primitive but real)
Profiler shows exactly where time is spent and how much native helps
643 tests at 100%, 970/1000 on public release protocol
still_honest_gaps: No external user validation (HG-10 — requires real humans)
No production case studies (research language, not yet deployed)
Performance claim: '27-80x faster' is real but context-dependent
Package ecosystem is minimal — 5 packages is not Python's 400k
